data.title: GreenLight Metals Inc. Announces the Closing of the Non-Brokered Private Placement of Units
data.description:
Medford, Wisconsin--(Newsfile Corp. - April 15, 2025) - GreenLight Metals Inc. (TSXV: GRL) ("GreenLight" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that further to its press release dated April 8, 2025 that it has closed a non-brokered private placement consisting of 1,650,000 units (the "Units") at a price of $0.30 per Unit for aggregate gross proceeds of $495,000.10 (the "Unit Offering"). Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of GreenLight (a "Share"), and one-half of one share purchase warrant in the capital of GreenLight (each whole warrant, a "Warrant"). The Warrants are governed by the terms of a warrant indenture dated January 30, 2025 between GreenLight and TSX Trust Company, among others. Each Warrant is exercisable by the holder thereof into one Share at a price of $0.45 until April 8, 2028.

The net proceeds from the Unit Offering will be used for the exploration of the Company's mineral projects, and for working capital and general corporate purposes. The Units will be subject to a statutory hold period pursuant to applicable Canadian securities laws.

No bonus, finder's fee, commission, agent options or other compensation was paid in connection with the Unit Offering.

In connection with the closing of the Unit Offering, 50,000 Units were subscribed for by a related party of the Company (as such term is defined in Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions ("MI 61-101")). The participation of the related party in the Unit Offering constitutes a "related party transaction" within the meaning of MI 61-101. The Company is relying on the exemptions from the valuation and minority shareholder approval requirements of MI 61-101 based on section 5.5(a) and 5.7(1)(a) of MI 61-101.

About GreenLight Metals Inc.

The principal business of GreenLight is the exploration and development of mineral properties in Wisconsin, Nevada, and Arizona. The material properties of GreenLight are the Bend Property, the Reef Property and the Kalium Canyon Property. In addition, the Company controls two additional prospective properties in Wisconsin - Lobo and Lobo East. GreenLight has also secured rights to the Swede anomaly located on the Southern Greenstone Belt. Outside of Wisconsin, the Company controls rights to the Kalium Canyon property, located in the Walker Lane district of Nevada. The Company also holds an option on the Cerro Colorado property located 70 kilometers southwest of Tucson, Arizona, along the Laramide porphyry copper belt.

Further Information

The Exchange has not in any way passed upon the merits of the Unit Offering and has neither approved nor disapproved the contents of this news release.

The securities have not been and will not be registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from the registration requirement. This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of the securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful.

For further information, please contact:

GREENLIGHT METALS INC.

Matt Filgate, President and Chief Executive Officer

Phone: (778) 679-3579

Email: matt@greenlightmetals.com

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

Forward Looking Information

Certain statements and information herein, including all statements that are not historical facts, contain forward-looking statements and forward-looking information within the meaning of applicable securities laws. Such forward-looking statements or information include but are not limited to statements or information with respect to: the use of proceeds in respect of the Unit Offering. Often, but not always, forward-looking statements or information can be identified by the use of words such as "anticipate", "believe", "estimate", "expect", "intend", "may", "plan", "predict", "project", "should" or "will" and the negative of these words or such variations thereon or comparable terminology are intended to identify forward-looking statements and information.

With respect to forward-looking statements and information contained herein, GreenLight has made numerous assumptions including among other things, assumptions about the general business and economic conditions of GreenLight and the market in which it operates. The foregoing list of assumptions is not exhaustive.

Although management of GreenLight believe that the assumptions made and the expectations represented by such statements or information are reasonable, there can be no assurance that forward-looking statements or information herein will prove to be accurate. Forward-looking statements and information by their nature are based on assumptions and involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors which may cause actual results, performance or achievements, or industry results, to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievements expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements or information. These factors include, but are not limited to: risks relating to the changes in interest and currency exchange rates; risks relating to unanticipated operational difficulties (including failure of technology or processes to operate in accordance with specifications or expectations, cost escalation, unavailability of materials and equipment, government action or delays in the receipt of government approvals, industrial disturbances or other job action, and unanticipated events related to health, safety and environmental matters); changes in general economic conditions or conditions in the financial markets; changes in laws; and other assumptions, risks, and uncertainties discussed in documents available under GreenLight's profile at www.sedarplus.ca. GreenLight does not undertake to update any forward-looking information, except in accordance with applicable securities laws.

NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWSWIRE SERVICES OR FOR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/248616

data.title: Gold Pushes Higher as Trade War Casualties MountMarket Talk
data.description: Gold rose 0.4% to $3,218.70 a troy ounce, the second-highest close in the front-month contract's history and making it 5 out of the past 6 sessions that gold has settled higher. The back-and-forth between the U.S. and China over tariffs continues to be the driving force for gold prices, with the investor focus remaining on safe havens. Today, China halted deliveries on jets built by Boeing — the latest volley in the ongoing trade war. Even with the dollar index rising, traders still turned toward gold. But a whipsaw turn lower is not out of the question in the short-term, says Rhona O'Connell of StoneX in a note. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: TSS, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
TSS, Inc., a leading provider of procurement and systems integration services, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing remarkable financial growth and strategic initiatives aimed at capitalizing on the burgeoning demand for AI-enabled computing infrastructure. The report highlights the company's financial performance, business achievements, strategic plans, and the challenges it faces in a competitive and rapidly evolving market.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $148.1 million, a 172% increase from 2023 driven by significant growth in procurement and systems integration businesses.
  • Gross Profit: $22.4 million, a 103% increase compared to 2023, mainly due to higher volumes of activity in procurement and systems integration businesses.
  • Income from Operations: $8.5 million, a 386% increase from 2023, reflecting effective leveraging of the expense structure.
  • Net Income: $6.0 million, a substantial increase from $0.1 million in 2023, driven by improved earnings from AI rack integration and procurement services.
  • Earnings per Common Share – Diluted: $0.24, reflecting the significant improvement in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company experienced significant growth in its procurement services, with revenues increasing by 205% in 2024. Systems integration services also saw a substantial increase of 157%, driven primarily by the integration of AI-enabled computer racks.
  • Sales Units: The company has been integrating AI-enabled computer racks since June 2024, with a multi-year agreement signed in October 2024 to continue this at a minimum weekly volume.
  • New Production Launches: A new production facility is being developed in Georgetown, Texas, with an investment of $25 million to $30 million to enhance electrical power and cooling capabilities, supporting both air-cooled and direct liquid-cooled racks.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued high demand for AI-enabled rack integration services, supported by a multi-year agreement with a major OEM customer. This agreement is expected to sustain elevated levels of activity and revenue for several years.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is focusing on expanding its direct selling resources and co-selling with partners to increase service portfolio penetration. It is also investing in operational excellence and cost competitiveness to enhance manufacturing capabilities.
  • Customer Focus: The company aims to strengthen customer relationships by focusing on cost-effective systems integration and lifecycle management, collaborating closely with its OEM partner to identify demand and design new services.
  • Strategic Partnerships: The company is pursuing selective strategic partnerships and acquisitions to expand service offerings and maximize market opportunities.
  • Employee Growth: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed 161 full-time employees and 43 temporary workers, primarily in rack integration services, highlighting the importance of attracting and retaining qualified personnel.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Multi-Year Agreement: TSS, Inc. has signed a multi-year agreement with its largest customer to provide systems integration services for AI-enabled computer racks, which includes maintaining a facility and staffing levels to support a minimum weekly volume of racks. This strategic initiative is aimed at capitalizing on the growing demand for AI and accelerated computing infrastructure.
  • Headquarters and Production Facility Move: The company is moving its headquarters and production facility to a new location to support increased production volumes, with planned capital expenditures of approximately $25 million to $30 million to enhance electrical power and cooling capabilities.
  • Capital Management: The company secured a construction loan from Susser Bank for up to $20 million, expandable to $25 million, to finance the build-out of the new facility. TSS, Inc. also engaged in share repurchases totaling $4.5 million to allow employees to satisfy tax withholding requirements and pay the exercise price upon the vesting of restricted stock and exercise of stock options. The company has filed a shelf registration statement on Form S-3, enabling it to raise up to $150 million from the capital markets if needed.
  • Future Outlook: TSS, Inc. anticipates that the multi-year agreement with its largest customer will provide sufficient revenue to cover the costs associated with the new facility and debt service. The company expects to continue its recent trend of operating profitably and reducing its accumulated deficit. Management believes that the company will remain profitable on a quarterly and annual basis in 2025, supported by the growth in AI rack integration services and improvements in supply chain constraints.

Challenges and Risks

  • Customer Concentration: The company heavily relies on a single OEM customer, which accounted for 99% and 96% of total revenues in 2024 and 2023, respectively. This concentration poses a significant risk if the customer reduces its business or terminates the relationship. The company is attempting to diversify its customer base but faces challenges in doing so.
  • Contractual Risks: Potential breach or termination of a multi-year rack integration agreement could lead to significant financial obligations without corresponding revenues.
  • Power Demands: Uncertainty in future power demands for AI-enabled computer racks could impact the company's ability to meet customer needs if additional power and cooling are not secured.
  • Tariffs and Supply Chain: U.S. imposed tariffs could negatively affect revenues and profitability due to increased costs and supply chain disruptions. Supply chain challenges continue to impact the integration business, causing delays and increased inventory holding costs.
  • Personnel Challenges: Difficulty in hiring and retaining qualified personnel, especially in competitive markets, could increase costs and impact business operations.
  • Operational Losses: A history of operating losses and uncertainty in sustaining profitability due to market volatility and global supply challenges.
  • Customer Base Diversification: The risk of not successfully diversifying the customer base, which remains heavily concentrated.
  • IT System Implementation: Implementation challenges with a new enterprise resource IT system could negatively impact operations.
  • Working Capital Fluctuations: Fluctuations in the procurement business requiring additional working capital.
  • Competitive Pressures: Competitive pressures in the industry could reduce growth opportunities and profitability.
  • Contract Cancellations: Most contracts are cancelable on short notice, posing a risk to revenue stability.
  • Security Breaches: Security breaches could lead to significant costs and reputational damage.

SEC Filing: TSS, Inc. [ TSSI ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp, a blank check company formed to effect an initial business combination, has released its Form 10-K report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business activities, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. The company is currently focused on completing a significant business combination with Btab Ecommerce Group, Inc., which is expected to transform its operational and financial landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $1.49 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, primarily driven by earnings on marketable securities held in the trust account.
  • Net Loss: $0.10 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, due to increased legal and accounting expenses, insurance expense amortization, and other administrative costs.
  • Interest Earned on Cash Held in the Trust Account: $1.97 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, contributing to non-operating income.
  • Earnings on Marketable Securities Held in the Trust Account: $3.88 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, significantly impacting net income.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp is a blank check company formed to effect an initial business combination, with recent activities focused on the Btab Business Combination.
  • Initial Public Offering: The company completed its IPO on December 13, 2021, raising $115 million through the sale of 11.5 million units, each consisting of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of a redeemable warrant.
  • Sponsor Handover: On November 8, 2023, a significant management change occurred with the transfer of sponsorship to Sriram Associates, LLC, which assumed various financial obligations and responsibilities.
  • Btab Business Combination: The company entered into a Business Combination Agreement with Btab Ecommerce Group, Inc., involving a two-step merger process to create a new entity, Btab Ecommerce Holdings, Inc.
  • Pre-Closing Recapitalization and Domestication: Prior to the business combination, the company will convert Class B shares to Class A shares and domesticate from the Cayman Islands to Delaware.
  • Transaction Consideration: The transaction involves Pubco issuing 25 million new shares valued at $250 million to Btab shareholders, with a mix of Class A and Class V shares.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates structuring the business combination to ensure the post-business combination entity owns or acquires a controlling interest in the target, with flexibility in using cash, debt, or equity for the transaction.
  • Operational Strategy: The company emphasizes a thorough due diligence process in evaluating target businesses, leveraging management's operational experience to identify strategic growth opportunities.
  • Regulatory Compliance: As an emerging growth company, Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp benefits from certain reporting exemptions, which it plans to utilize until it no longer qualifies under the JOBS Act criteria.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on completing the Btab Business Combination, which involves merging with Btab Ecommerce Group, Inc. This strategic initiative is aimed at transforming the company into a wholly owned subsidiary of a new entity, Pubco, which will be renamed Btab Ecommerce Holdings, Inc. The company has also extended the deadline for consummating an initial business combination to December 15, 2025, to allow more time for this strategic transition.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including the issuance of promissory notes to fund extensions and operational costs. The company raised $115 million through its initial public offering and an additional $6.85 million from the sale of private placement warrants. It has also managed its liquidity by transferring trust account funds to Morgan Stanley Smith Barney LLC. The company has faced significant redemptions, with $36.7 million removed from the trust account in 2024. Additionally, the company has no long-term debt obligations but has deferred underwriting fees of $4.025 million payable upon completion of the business combination.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates incurring significant costs in pursuing the Btab Business Combination and acknowledges substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. It plans to raise additional funds to meet operational expenditures and complete the business combination. The company is also exploring the possibility of converting up to $1.5 million of promissory notes into ordinary shares upon the consummation of the business combination, which could provide additional capital flexibility.

Challenges and Risks

  • Challenges in Completing Business Combination: Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp faces significant challenges in completing its initial business combination due to the 2024 SPAC Rules, which may increase costs and time required for negotiations. The company also faces competition from other SPACs and entities seeking similar business combinations, which could make it more difficult to identify and secure a suitable target.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company acknowledges cybersecurity risks, particularly due to its reliance on third-party digital technologies and lack of significant investments in data security. This vulnerability could lead to material adverse consequences and financial loss if a cybersecurity incident occurs.
  • Extended Deadline for Business Combination: The company has extended its deadline for completing an initial business combination multiple times, now set for December 15, 2025. This extension reflects challenges in identifying and securing a suitable target, compounded by increased competition in the SPAC market. The company must also manage the risk of shareholder redemptions, which could impact its ability to maintain sufficient net tangible assets.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks related to its ability to complete a business combination within the specified timeframe. Failure to do so could result in liquidation and the return of funds to shareholders, which would mean the company would not realize any potential gains from a successful business combination. Additionally, the company must navigate regulatory risks associated with the SPAC framework and potential changes in market conditions that could affect its operations and financial stability.

SEC Filing: Integrated Wellness Acquisition Corp [ WEL ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: Coffee rallies on tight stocks, raw sugar hits 2-1/2 year lows
data.description:
Coffee prices rose on Tuesday as investor focus returned to extremely tight stocks and relief in wider markets after U.S. President Donald Trump's latest changes in tariff policy , while raw sugar hit the lowest price in more than two years.

Investors remain concerned, however, that a global trade war could weaken economic growth.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee futures traded on the ICE exchange settled up 8.65 cents, or 2.4%, at $3.6715 per lb, having lost 2.7% last week. Robusta coffee futures rose 1.9% to $5,340 a metric ton, having declined by 1.5% last week.

* Consultancy Safras & Mercado said on Tuesday that Brazil's production could be a bit bigger than expected in 2025. It, however, estimates farmers have sold only 14% of the new crop versus a 25% long-term average for the period, citing uncertainty over production prospects.

* A dealer said he expects arabica to recover to a range of $3.60 to $3.80 per lb this week, given supply jitters and tight stocks.

COCOA

* London cocoa futures settled down 168 pounds, or 2.8%, to 5,805 pounds per ton. New York cocoa fell 2.9% to $7,926 a ton.

* Cocoa investors continue to fear a downturn in demand for the chocolate ingredient, with first-quarter cocoa grind data - a measure of demand - likely to show steep falls of 5% to 7%, dealers said.

* Brazil cocoa grind fell 13% in the first quarter.

* Most areas of Ivory Coast, Cameroon and Nigeria are expected to receive beneficial rain over the next week to 10 days.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar settled down 0.35 cent, or 2%, at 17.52 cents per lb, having hit a 2-1/2 year low of 17.51, while white sugar fell 2.7% at $513.10 a ton.

* "Uncertain demand driven by an uncertain global economic outlook remains a concern," said broker ADMISI.

* Sugar production in top producer Brazil's centre-south region was nearly 10% higher than a year earlier in the second half of March as mills allocated more cane to sugar production.

* Elsewhere, France's farm ministry estimated the sugar beet area for this year's harvest at 391,000 hectares, down from 412,000 hectares in 2024 and 2.9% below the five-year average.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day. 1437ET - China's halting of new orders of Boeing aircraft is likely unsustainable, Bank of America analyst Ronald Epstein says. China can rely on its leased aircraft inventory for a while, but not forever, and its domestically based planemaker Comac is heavily dependent on parts from the U.S. European rival Airbus can gain some share but can't realistically fill Boeing's shoes as China's largest jet supplier due to capacity constraints, he says. Even if this cut-off is prolonged, Boeing should be able to reallocate its aircraft supply to other airlines in other countries such as India, Epstein says. Boeing shares fall 2% to $156.52. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1429 ET - U.S. cyber insurance renewal premium rates, which have fallen year-over-year for the last three quarters, are expected to remain under pressure near-term, barring a significant event, Fitch Ratings says. "The cyber insurance line was profitable for 2024, but direct written premiums fell for the second straight year due to declining rates and fewer policies in force," according to Fitch. Ongoing challenges include intensifying market competition, as well as "an evolving claims environment increasingly influenced by technological changes, including artificial intelligence, rather than regulations or case law," Fitch adds. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1408 ET - Dick's Sporting Goods is well positioned to gain market share in the current macroeconomic environment, Truist Securities analysts say in a research note. The country's largest sporting-goods retailer can also offset tariff headwinds through modest price increases, given that its customers are relatively cushioned enough to absorb the impact, the analysts say. Overall, Dick's Sporting Goods offers brands a chance to engage with a more affluent and engaged customer-base who has shopped loyally and at full-price. Furthermore, the company has ample flexibility to cut spending if needed, the analysts add. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1400 ET - Software companies are expected to face a slowdown in activity from enterprises as they seek to preserve cash in the current environment, BofA Securities analysts say in a research note. While tariffs pose little direct impact to the software industry, with the exception of eCommerce companies such as Shopify, the cyclical nature of software project approvals represents a threat, the analysts say. BofA lowers its 2Q and 2025 estimates 1% and 4% across the software industry. With the software group off 18% from February 18, the analysts say they are also lowering price targets on stocks. "While positive tariff-related news flow could drive a step-change recovery of multiples (as seen following the 90-day tariff pause headline), we believe it's prudent to adjust price objectives based on the environment we are in today." (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1343 ET - Lululemon is losing the market share battle to Alo Yoga in Miami, according to Jefferies analysts in a note, and it's an ominous sign for LuLulemon's prospects across the rest of the country. The analysts say fashion trends often start on the coast and move inward, and they believe Lululemon's non-core products aren't selling and "newness" isn't resonating with customers. Meanwhile, the analysts say Alo's lifestyle-oriented approach is seeing success, evidenced by the popularity of its tops, bottoms, and socks in Miami, where they say a new Alo location is set to open in the same shopping center as Lululemon. The situation has Jefferies, which has an underperform on the stock, anticipating lower sales and earnings for Lululemon in the U.S. this year. Lululemon is off 1.7% and down 24% in the past 12 months. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1243 ET - The market for U.S. beer, flavored malt beverages and cider saw year-over-year volume declines of 4.6% over the past 12 months, a softening that was likely exacerbated by the later timing of Easter this year, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. Easter arrived at the end of March in 2024 but is happening on April 20 this year, meaning the comparable period at issue doesn't have the Easter boost it received last year, they say. This makes for a sequential decline from the already weak volumes the market experienced in early March and "the lowest level seen over the last 12 months," the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1236 ET - U.S. beverages and household and personal care companies will report challenging 1Q results, Citi analysts say in a research note. The companies aren't only facing a difficult set-up for the sector with soft commentary on the U.S. and Europe, but also from key emerging markets such as Mexico and India. Both markets had been positive elements of growth for the companies in the past few quarters. Many haven't provided commentary on tariffs, so that will be a large focus. Citi has a cautious near-term view on Procter & Gamble, Colgate-Palmolive and Coca-Cola, and a positive view on Church & Dwight, Boston Beer and Keurig Dr Pepper. Citi cuts its target price for the stocks of Estee Lauder, Newell Brands, Clorox and Brown-Forman, and raises its target price on Boston Beer, Monster Beverage and Kenvue. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1206 ET - Mullen Group's acquisition of Cole Group is a good way to branch out into less tariff-stricken areas, TD Cowen's Tim James says. In a report, the analyst rates the acquisition as "slightly positive based on the exposure it provides to supply chain and international commercial trade services." James figures that with the evolving tariff and counter-tariff policies in Canada and the U.S., the acquisition of an asset-light logistics company specializing in customs brokerage and trade consulting is likely a prudent move to take advantage of the changes in trade policies. While it is still unclear how much exposure Cole has to cross-border trade, James thinks that advisory/consulting services will be helpful in these times. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1153 ET - Bank of America expects the Trump administration's agenda to result in fewer or more refined regulations, as well as a smaller federal government that will mean fewer regulatory inquiries and related matters. Executives on an earnings call say that sometimes regulations get in the way and overshoot the issues they oversee, but that they're looking forward to having them come back to the middle. They add that they look forward to seeing more relief as the administration's nominees get into position. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1137 ET - Bank of America downgrades Dow to underperform from buy, saying the chemical company faces significant earnings headwinds from an economic slowdown and intensifying trade war that could put its rich dividend at risk. In a note, BofA says Dow faces a "perfect storm" of softening macro, emerging barriers to trade and higher U.S. feedstock costs, and the analysts slash their 2025 and 2026 Ebitda estimates by 17% and 23%, respectively. BofA also says Dow faces a significant free-cash-flow deficit in funding its $2 billion annual dividend, which currently offers a nearly 10% yield. BofA cuts its Dow price objective to $28 from $44. Dow down 2.2% to $28.24. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

1133 ET - Bank of America stuck to a familiar formula when calculating its loan loss reserves. The bank's provision for credit losses of $1.5 billion in 1Q25 was flat with provisions from 4Q24. Executives on an earnings call said as they have in prior quarters, they came to their figure using a model involving blue chip economic indicators which they feel is more independent. Using those indicators as a baseline, they also planned for four scenarios - three of which were downside scenarios. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1121 ET - Argentina's lifting of capital controls will likely help attract international investors to the country, as they will feel more confident they can take their money out when they need to, according to UBS. Argentina's government last week announced the end of most restrictions to the flow of foreign currencies in and out of the country, while introducing a more flexible exchange rate system that let the Argentine peso fluctuate within a certain range. A new, $20 billion credit facility by the International Monetary Fund will support the initiative. "The new [currency] regime should allow the peso to adjust and regain competitiveness, with continued disinflation efforts falling more squarely on the shoulders of more active monetary policy," the investment bank says. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

data.title: China's Boeing Cut-Off is UnsustainableMarket Talk

China can rely on its leased aircraft inventory for a while, but not forever, and its domestically based planemaker Comac is heavily dependent on parts from the U.S. European rival Airbus can gain some share but can't realistically fill Boeing's shoes as China's largest jet supplier due to capacity constraints, he says. Even if this cut-off is prolonged, Boeing should be able to reallocate its aircraft supply to other airlines in other countries such as India, Epstein says. Boeing shares fall 2% to $156.52. data.description: General Mills has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $62.39, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - Some trade policy relief and strong bank earnings helped push Wall Street up slightly on Tuesday, while U.S. government bonds and the dollar were steady, after U.S. President Donald Trump touted possible tariff changes on autos.

In Asia, MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) gained 1.1%.

- - - -

The pan-European STOXX 600 gained 1.6%, with most regional indexes in positive territory.

The Nikkei ended the day up 0.84% at 34,267.54.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China and Hong Kong stocks were flat on Tuesday, as investors awaited more clarity on the trade situation after the Trump administration stepped up probes into semiconductor imports.

China's blue-chip CSI300 Index climbed less than 0.1% and the Shanghai Composite Index added 0.2% at close, after both traded in a tight range during the day.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares edged higher on Tuesday, buoyed by a global relief rally and gains in major banks, though caution lingered ahead of the country's upcoming federal election.

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 0.2% to 7,761.7 at the close of trade.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares rose for a second straight session on Tuesday, as automakers jumped after U.S. President Donald Trump floated exemptions to auto tariffs.

The benchmark KOSPI closed up 21.52 points, or 0.88%, at 2,477.41.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar stabilised on Tuesday, edging up from a three-year low against the euro as risk sentiment improved, but also held near a six-month trough against the yen as investors worried about the impact of U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade tariffs on the U.S. economy.

The euro was last down 0.28% on the day at $1.1317, just below last week's three-year high at $1.1473.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China's yuan held steady against the U.S. dollar on Tuesday even as markets grappled with the Trump administration's back-and forth changes in tariffs.

The spot yuan opened at 7.3050 per dollar and was last trading at 7.3086 as of 0306 GMT, 4 pips firmer than the previous late session close and 1.37% weaker than the midpoint.

The Aussie has made it back to $0.6353 , up from a $0.5910.

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - Yields on U.S. Treasury debt were little changed early on Tuesday as traders marked time before midweek data on consumer activity and a speech from the head of the Federal Reserve, with little new on the tariff front to rekindle last week's bond rout.

The 10-year yield (US10YT=TWEB) was unchanged from late Monday at 4.364%.

data.title: Southeast Toyota Finance Announces Leadership Changes and New Initiative to Drive Future Growth
data.description:
Deerfield Beach, Fla., April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Dan Chait, president and CEO of JM Family Enterprises, announced executive leadership changes for subsidiary Southeast Toyota Finance, a captive financial services company driven to delivering an exceptional experience for Toyota customers in the southeast. These leadership appointments come as Southeast Toyota Finance continues its journey of innovation and transformation through SPARK, a strategic initiative designed to modernize operations, enhance dealer and customer experiences, and position the company for long-term success. 

After 35 impactful years with the company, former president Bill Shope retired at the end of March. Shope's remarkable journey and contributions have left a lasting legacy, including advancing analytic capabilities, strengthening dealer relationships, and launching the SPARK business transformation project, which includes innovative initiatives that will propel the company forward in 2025 and beyond. 

In a strategic move to continue this momentum, Casey Gunnell Jr., most recently GVP of Southeast Toyota Finance, was named president. Gunnell's 27-year tenure with JM Family, including 25 years at sister company Southeast Toyota Distributors, has been marked by significant achievements in various leadership roles. His commitment to the company's values and associates ensures that Southeast Toyota Finance will continue to thrive under his leadership. 

"Casey's promotion to president of Southeast Toyota Finance recognizes and celebrates his nearly three decades of driving success across our automotive and finance operations," said Chait. “He's a proven and capable leader who understands that strong results come from investing in people as much as they come from strategic agility and execution. I am confident that under Casey's leadership, Southeast Toyota Finance will continue to set industry standards and deliver superior service to our customers."  

Additionally, Southeast Toyota Finance executives announced the promotions of Charles Gruszczynski and Michael Hollis to Group Vice President (GVP). These promotions are closely tied to the company's SPARK initiatives, ensuring a seamless alignment of leadership and strategic goals. 

Gruszczynski, now GVP of Portfolio Management, will oversee Analytics, Data, Portfolio Finance, Risk, and Credit Administration. His leadership will be instrumental in driving SPARK’s Technology & Process initiative, leveraging advanced technology and data to enhance efficiency and deliver flexible solutions.  

Hollis, as GVP of Operations, will lead strategies and operations within the Customer Experience Centers and manage strategic partnerships. His role will be pivotal in the Organizational initiative, developing a future-ready workforce equipped with the skills to thrive in a rapidly changing environment. 

Ranked No. 1 in dealer satisfaction among Captive Mass Market-Prime Automotive Finance Lenders for the second year in a row by the J.D. Power 2024 U.S. Dealer Financing Satisfaction Study, Southeast Toyota Finance launches the next evolution of the SPARK program, focusing on four key initiatives to continue exceeding the evolving needs of dealers and customers. 

    • Transaction: Evolving programs and the customer experience to position Southeast Toyota Finance as the primary consideration in-store and online. 
    • Vehicle: Creating competitive financing solutions for evolving vehicles and associated technology to achieve market leadership and customer retention. 
    • Technology & Process: Leveraging advanced technology and process improvements to enhance efficiency and deliver flexible solutions. 
    • Organizational: Developing a future-ready workforce equipped with the skills to thrive in a rapidly changing environment. 

“This is an excellent leadership team in place as Southeast Toyota Finance continues to grow,” Chait continued. “We look forward to the continued success that their collective efforts will bring to our organization and dealers.” 

These initiatives are crucial as Southeast Toyota Finance aligns its efforts around a shared vision for the future. Together, the company will continue to drive success and innovation, ensuring Southeast Toyota Finance remains a leader in the industry. 

About Southeast Toyota Finance 

Southeast Toyota Finance (part of World Omni Financial Corp.) is a captive financial services company driven to delivering an exceptional dealership and customer experience for Toyota customers in the southeast. The company offers a broad range of products and services to the 177 Toyota dealers in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina. Southeast Toyota Finance is the leading provider of finance and lease options for new Toyota vehicles within its footprint, consistently ranking at the top in market share and dealer satisfaction. The company is committed to strong dealer relationships and customer service, providing finance, retail and lease programs, college graduate and military rebate programs that drive customer and dealer loyalty. The company’s portfolio of dealer loans includes inventory financing, real estate, and construction loans. 

Southeast Toyota Finance is a subsidiary of JM Family Enterprises, Inc., a privately held company with more than $22 billion in revenue and more than 5,000 associates, which is headquartered in Deerfield Beach, Florida. 

Attachment

  • Casey Gunnell Jr., Charles Gruszczynski and Michael Hollis

Allison Collett

Southeast Toyota Finance

(954) 254-8025

allison.collett@setoyota.com

data.title: 'Term Premium' Sends a Message. Inflation Isn't What Bond Investors Fear Most.
data.description:
Karishma Vanjani

An unusual situation has unfolded in the U.S. Treasury market, where investors are expecting lower inflation in the distant future, yet demanding better returns to buy and hold bonds.

The yield on U.S. government debt maturing in 10 years slipped by 0.021 percentage point to 4.36% on Tuesday, calming down after marking its biggest weekly gain since the end of the 2001 recession last week. Sales of U.S. debt are pushing down prices and raising yields; yields and bond prices are inversely related.

Last week, Wall Street mainly blamed the surge on foreign central banks selling bonds and the unwinding of a complex, highly leveraged hedge fund strategy called the basis trade. Both were difficult-to-measure variables, partly because transactions by both groups are opaque.

Now, market watchers are looking at the s0-called 10-year Treasury term premium. It measures the extra yield investors demand to stash away money for a decade, rather than just repeatedly investing into short-term securities, such as one-month Treasury bills, for 10 years. It makes sense that investors should get more yield for locking up their money for longer because there is a higher chance that interest rates, or other important variables, would change.

How high the premium goes reflects how much risk investors see. And right now, they see plenty. The latest available figure for the premium, from Thursday, has it at 0.7052 percentage point, the highest since September 2014.

So far this month, it has risen 0.33 point, the largest increase in the first eight trading days of a month since April 2022, the Dow Jones Market Data team says. The yield climbed markedly last week, coinciding with the back-and-forth on President Donald Trump's tariffs and drama in Congress as Republicans seek to raise the cap on the federal debt.

"Perhaps the likelihood of a new debt ceiling bill that blows out the deficit plus policy uncertainty are raising the term premium on bonds," Ed Yardeni, president of Yardeni Research, a well respected independent research firm, wrote on Monday.

The term premium was also blamed for a rise in yields in January as well as distress in the bond market in late 2023.

What is notable this time is that the rise in term premium has come as the market's expectations for inflation have stayed low. The 10-year break-even inflation rate, which indicates the market's expectations for inflation, closed at 2.23% on Monday, after falling to 2.18% on Friday, the lowest in more than six months. It signals that yields are rising because of factors other than long-term inflation expectations.

"The backup in yields was entirely due to the rapid increase in the term premium...and actually not one iota from inflation expectations," wrote David Rosenberg, one of the more bearish strategists on Wall Street.

Higher yields spell opportunity for investors waiting on the sidelines for better rates, but they are a menace nonetheless. They raise borrowing costs for consumers, via mortgage and credit-card rates, and for corporate borrowers. That leaves the public with less money to spend, and boosts costs for companies, with negative implications for stocks and economic growth.

Write to Karishma Vanjani at karishma.vanjani@dowjones.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Honda Says Canadian Auto Production Not Moving Out, Denies Earlier Reports
data.description:
Honda Motor said Tuesday it will not be moving any production from Canada to the US.

Nikkei reported earlier that the automaker was considering to move some of its production out of Canada and Mexico to avoid tariffs by the US government.

In its statement, Honda Canada said it "can confirm that our Canadian manufacturing facility in Alliston, Ontario, will operate at full capacity for the foreseeable future and no changes are being considered at this time."

Honda Canada declined to comment on specifics of the reports, although it said it "constantly" reviews its options to address future contingencies and will occasionally elects temporary production strategy shifts from one facility to another "to mitigate negative impacts on [its] business."

Anita Anand, Canadian industry minister, citing conversations with company officials, also said on Tuesday the report was false. "Honda has communicated that no such production decisions affecting Canadian operations have been made, and are not being considered at this time," she posted on X.

Measured by volume, Honda said it was the second largest auto manufacturer in Canada during 2024, with about 69% of the vehicles it sold in Canada produced in the country.

Honda shares recently were up more than 1%.

data.title: RoofBids Launches New Online Roofing Platform
data.description:
Winter Garden, Florida, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — RoofBids is changing the roofing process for homeowners by launching its new online roofing platform. This new one-stop shop, which launches in May, will allow you to get your roofing project done by finding the most trusted roof contractors in your local area.

Roofing projects can be painful for many homeowners, with the number one issue being that so many get scammed. Much like an auto repair shop, an inherent element of trust must be part of an owner's relationship with a roofer. You may not be a roofing expert, but that doesn't mean you have to allow yourself to be vulnerable and taken advantage of by nefarious parties. 

Over 50% of all roof-related projects will have workmanship issues that must be addressed after the initial work is completed. Thus, you need a team you can trust to deliver outstanding work and reliable service afterward.

In the current roofing ecosystem, it is far too easy for homeowners to fall victim to malicious malpractice. Over 80% of new roof contractors leave the business within three years. This indicates the quality of work they deliver and has even more dire ramifications for those served: the contractor's closure leaves them with no warranty recourse.

The Benefits of RoofBids

RoofBids’ new online platform helps homeowners navigate this world, giving them access to the most trusted roof contractors in their local area. Every contractor featured on the platform has been fully vetted through the company's twenty-plus-point application and review process. 

RoofBids limits its partners so you don't get inundated with random contractors. With its new online platform, there are no aggressive sales pitches, just a simple online process. With RoofBids, you get 2-3 quotes on your dashboard, make the selection, and get your project ready to start.

The Origins of RoofBids

Joe Casalese is the Chief Connections Officer and founder of RoofBids, a Central Florida-based company revolutionizing the roofing industry with a new digital platform to connect homeowners with trusted and vetted roofing contractors. RoofBids will launch in May to eliminate the common frustrations, scams, and inefficiencies afflicting the home improvement and roofing industries.

RoofBids was born from a clear need. Soon after Joe joined the industry and became disillusioned by its questionable practices. "We were taking advantage of so many people who were uninformed," he recalls. 

This experience led him to create RoofBids, a platform driven by integrity and trust designed to advocate for property owners.

Lesson Learned

The roofing industry has been built on the back of practices that take advantage of and ultimately leave the homeowner at a loss. From scams to high-pressure sales to false insurance claims to overinflated insurance claims to payments made directly to roof contractors instead of the homeowners, these questionable practices have taken advantage of people for too long. Now, RoofBids is striving to set all of that straight.

A Brighter Future

Looking ahead, RoofBids aims to restore trust in an industry where it's been lacking for too long. Joe envisions a future where homeowners feel confident and at peace when taking on roofing projects, knowing they have access to reliable, vetted professionals.

With continued growth, RoofBids may expand into other home improvement segments, bringing the same commitment to integrity, transparency, and simplicity that it brings to roofing today.

About RoofBids

RoofBids is disrupting the roofing industry the way Zillow disrupted the realtor industry. Both industries have a low net promoter score, indicating a lack of trust and service. RoofBids is a change agent, not a roof contractor, and an opportunity to understand what their roof project will take and who to trust to get it done right.

Chris K.

S99 Agency

ck@s99agency.com

data.title: NZDUSD retests 200-day moving average after first break above since October 2024
data.description:
NZDUSD technicals

The NZDUSD has made a significant technical move today, climbing above the 200-day moving average for the first time since October 9, 2024. This long-term moving average, currently sitting at 0.5888, had held resistance on a couple of tests yesterday before breaking above the in the Asian Pacific session today:

  • Earlier today, price surged above the 200-day MA, a key bullish signal.

  • The rally followed a breakout above the 100-day MA and a sharp upside momentum shift last week. The 100 bar MA on the 4-hour chart, the 200 bar MA on the 4-hour chart and that 100 day MA were all broken near the same level.

  • The pair is now in the process of retesting the 200-day MA from above — a critical zone to determine whether buyers can maintain control.

  • A hold above 0.5888 keeps the bullish bias alive and opens the door to further upside toward the 0.5930 level and beyond.

  • A move back below the 200-day MA would be a setback and could shift momentum back to sellers

Key levels:

  • Support: 0.5888 (200-day MA), 0.5852 High from 2-weeks ago. 0.5824 (broken 38.2% retracement of the move down from September 2024

  • Resistance: 0.5930 (50% retracement), 0.5970 to 0.5983 (swing area going back in time) 0.6000 (natural resistance)

data.title: Automotive Properties REIT Announces April 2025 Distribution
data.description:
/NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWS WIRE SERVICES OR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES/

TORONTO, April 15, 2025 /CNW/ - Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (the "REIT") today announced a cash distribution of $0.067 per REIT unit for the month of April 2025, representing $0.804 per REIT unit on an annualized basis. Payment will be made on May 15, 2025 to unitholders of record as at April 30, 2025.

About Automotive Properties REIT

Automotive Properties REIT is an unincorporated, open-ended real estate investment trust focused on owning and acquiring primarily income-producing automotive and other OEM dealership and service properties located in Canada and the United States. The REIT's portfolio currently consists of 80 income-producing commercial properties, representing approximately three million square feet of gross leasable area, in metropolitan markets across British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario and Québec in Canada, and Florida and Ohio in the United States. Automotive Properties REIT is the only public vehicle in Canada focused on consolidating automotive and OEM dealership and service real estate properties. For more information, please visit: www.automotivepropertiesreit.ca.

SOURCE Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust

View original content: http://www.newswire.ca/en/releases/archive/April2025/15/c5100.html

/CONTACT:

data.title: Cyber Insurance Premium Rates Expected to Feel PressureMarket Talk
data.description: U.S. cyber insurance renewal premium rates, which have fallen year-over-year for the last three quarters, are expected to remain under pressure near-term, barring a significant event, Fitch Ratings says. "The cyber insurance line was profitable for 2024, but direct written premiums fell for the second straight year due to declining rates and fewer policies in force," according to Fitch. Ongoing challenges include intensifying market competition, as well as "an evolving claims environment increasingly influenced by technological changes, including artificial intelligence, rather than regulations or case law," Fitch adds. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

data.title: National Bank on March Airfares
data.description:
Airfares in Canada through the first three months of the year continue to be relatively weak with the March reading declining year-on-year for the ninth straight month, notes National Bank.

However, March fares were ahead of 2019 levels for the seventh straight month. When reporting its fiscal first-quarter (ended January) results in mid-March, Transat indicated that so far in fiscal Q2 (the peak winter quarter), yields were up 2%. Transat management added that winter trends (of modestly higher yields) seemed to be continuing into the summer.

While a shift of some U.S. transborder capacity into the domestic Canada market could pressure domestic yields in the coming quarters, National Bank notes that trans-Atlantic capacity growth looks to be more modest this summer. Early fare surveys for several key trans-Atlantic markets during the peak travel period show average fares largely up relative to last summer, analyst Cameron Doerksen writes.

Air Canada is rated Outperform, with a $23 target.

Transat is rated Underperform, with a $1.50 target.
